differential_diagnoses: Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (secondary to dapagliflozin use)
Pyroglutamic acidosis (5-oxoprolinuria due to chronic acetaminophen and nutritional depletion)
Acute-on-chronic heart failure leading to hypoperfusion and Type A lactic acidosis
Toxic alcohol ingestion (ethylene glycol or methanol)
next_steps: Measure arterial blood gas to confirm pH and lactate levels
Calculate the anion gap and serum osmolar gap
Send serum for organic acid profile and salicylate/toxic alcohol levels
Perform arterial/venous blood gas comparison to evaluate for possible uremia or underlying systemic acidosis
Continue aggressive fluid optimization and evaluate for hemodialysis if severe MALA or toxic ingestion is confirmed

differential_diagnoses: Euglycemic diabetic ketoacidosis (secondary to SGLT2 inhibitor)
Acute-on-chronic heart failure with hypoperfusion (cardiogenic shock)
next_steps: Obtain arterial blood gas with serum lactate, electrolytes, BUN, and creatinine
Calculate the anion gap and determine the delta-delta ratio
Measure serum beta-hydroxybutyrate to screen for ketoacidosis
Check serum cortisol level to evaluate for adrenal insufficiency
Review trending serum creatinine to assess for acute kidney injury
Evaluate for source of infection given history of urinary symptoms
Monitor fluid balance closely while balancing diuresis and hemodynamic support
